purpose: Provides observable, real-time market signals for tracking fiscal dominance progression and distinguishing gradual from acute pathways.
components: ACM term premium level (currently +50-100bp) and velocity (<50bp/quarter = gradual, >100bp/quarter = acute)
Indirect bidder share at Treasury auctions (currently ~65%, down from 72%)
Stock-bond correlation bifurcation (2Y-SPX negative vs. 30Y-SPX positive)
current_assessment: All indicators are in the early/gradual zone. Term premium velocity is below the acute threshold. Auction metrics are deteriorating but not at crisis levels. Breakevens remain well-anchored. The correlation bifurcation is emerging.
limitations: Post-QE structural break makes pre-QE regression coefficients unreliable
QT-era sample (~3 years) is too short for reliable re-estimation
Specific threshold values (e.g., 150bp term premium) carry 4-5/10 confidence
No single indicator is sufficient — requires composite assessment
